articles.title: 5 Takeaways From the ‘No Kings’ Rallies as the Midterms Heat Up
articles.summary: The war in Iran was a galvanizing force, but plenty of protesters focused on President Trump’s immigration crackdown. Senate candidates in several key races joined the crowds.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

articles.title: ‘No Kings’ Protests Decry Trump and His Agenda
articles.summary: Thousands of organized demonstrations stretched across the country. Minnesota was a focal point of the protests after a tumultuous immigration crackdown.

articles.title: How to navigate the maze of drug discounts to get the best price
articles.summary: In February, TrumpRx joined a growing list of websites consumers can tap for discounts on their medicines. Here's a cheat sheet for getting the best deal.

articles.title: Record Number of T.S.A. Employees Called Out on Friday
articles.summary: President Trump signed a memo late Friday ordering the Department of Homeland Security to restore pay to airport screeners.

articles.title: Pakistan hosts diplomatic discussions on ending war
articles.summary: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt will meet in Islamabad today in an attempt to come up with a plan to de-escalate the Iran war, after another group got involved in the e

articles.title: Why a 98-year-old federal judge is asking the Supreme Court for her job back
articles.summary: Pauline Newman's story shines a light on the aging judiciary, where judges are getting older and lifetime tenure is raising thorny questions about retirement.

articles.title: No Kings Protests Held Across the U.S.: Photos and Videos
articles.summary: It’s the third time that the coalition behind the “No Kings” movement has organized events to protest President Trump and his policies. In the United States, more than 3,000 demonstrations were planne

articles.title: Deaths in ICE Custody Are Growing. ‘They Let Him Rot in There.’
articles.summary: As immigrant detainee deaths have increased, conditions in detention facilities nationwide are coming under more scrutiny.

articles.title: Some critics of birthright citizenship say it's a fraud issue. What does that mean?
articles.summary: Advocates for ending birthright citizenship point to "birth tourism" schemes to argue that the legal principle is ripe for exploitation and threatens national security. Experts say it's not so simple.

articles.title: He wants children's bikes made in the U.S.A. — and tariffs against his rivals
articles.summary: Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.

articles.title: A Toothless Iran? Missile and Drone Strikes Show It Can Still Inflict Pain.
articles.summary: A wave of strikes across the Middle East in recent days shows that Iran has not lost the capacity to retaliate.

articles.title: Photos: 'No Kings' protests across the country
articles.summary: People showed up for rallies in more than 3,000 communities from coast to coast on Saturday, to vent their frustration and decry the policies of the Trump administration.

articles.title: G.O.P. Rift Leaves Congress With No Clear Path to End the Shutdown
articles.summary: The deadlock that left the Department of Homeland Security shuttered highlighted Republican divisions that are flaring ahead of the midterm elections.

articles.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
articles.summary: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

articles.title: Yemen’s Houthis Fire at Israel and Vow Further Attacks
articles.summary: There has long been concern that the Houthis, an Iran-backed militia, could disrupt shipping in the Red Sea if they entered the broader war.

articles.title: Opinion: White House 'gamifying' Iran war updates
articles.summary: The White House has depicted the war in Iran online with videos that weave real life images of missile strikes and destruction with clips from video games, sports clips, and action movies.

articles.title: The iconic South African theater that took on apartheid
articles.summary: South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

articles.title: Over a dozen U.S. soldiers injured in attack on Saudi base as Iran-backed Houthis enter war
articles.summary: An Iranian strike on an air base in Saudi Arabia wounded at least 15 U.S. service members. Israel also said it intercepted a missile launched from Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen.

articles.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
articles.summary: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.

articles.title: How long will the war last? No one knows, and it's making oil prices weird
articles.summary: It's like the "Schrödinger's cat" thought experiment. There are two very different potential realities, and traders don't yet know which one is true.

articles.title: Americans seek redemption at figure skating worlds, just weeks after the Olympics
articles.summary: Figure Skating World Championships in Prague end on Saturday. Americans Amber Glenn and Ilia Malinin are within medals' reach after disappointing finishes at last month's Olympics.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
